source.citation: Young K, Fisher J, Kirkman M. Women's experiences of endometriosis: a systematic review and synthesis of qualitative research. J Fam Plann Reprod Health Care. 2015 Jul;41(3):225-234.
source.note: Themes encoded from the abstract + key findings; refine if full text obtained.

themes.label: Diagnostic delay and dismissal
themes.description: Women describe long delays before diagnosis, often with their pain dismissed or normalized by clinicians.

themes.label: Living with chronic pain
themes.description: Endometriosis pain is chronic, debilitating, and shapes daily life, work, and relationships.

themes.label: Emotional and psychological impact
themes.description: Patients report depression, anxiety, frustration, and a sense of being unheard within healthcare.

themes.label: Treatment disappointment and side effects
themes.description: Hormonal therapies and surgeries provide variable, often temporary relief and carry significant side effects; pregnancy is sometimes presented as a treatment, which patients reject.

themes.label: Effect on relationships and intimacy
themes.description: The condition strains romantic, family, and social relationships, including impacts on sexual intimacy and fertility.

themes.label: Fertility and reproductive concerns
themes.description: Worry about future fertility and reproductive choices is a major psychological burden.

themes.label: Information-seeking and self-advocacy
themes.description: Women actively seek information online and from peers, often becoming experts on their own condition due to perceived gaps in clinician knowledge.

themes.label: Online and peer support communities
themes.description: Online communities and peer support are valued sources of validation, information, and coping.
